授業目的: A KALEIDOSCOPE OF JAPANESE CINEMAThis course intends to introduce students to the fascinating variety of Japanese cinema through a number of films representing different genres, themes, and directors, including anime, and explore ways in which cinema offers a perspective into history, society, and culture.
到達目標: The main objective of this course is to help students deepen their understanding of Japanese cinema and provide them with a basic scholarship to discuss one of the world's most important film cultures.
特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（映画論Ａ）（CIEC）/Japanese Cinema A（国際学部）
関連科目:
Classic Japanese Cinema
Japanese Pop Culture
Traditional Japanese Theatre
Japanese culture, society, and history
Gender Perspectives in Japanese Cinema
Transmedia
授業方法: Classes will be conducted face-to-face.Every class will use materials and homework relevant to the week's lesson and contents together with explanations through .doc/ pdf / mp4 files.The instructor will also provide relevant internet links for study and self-study.Students will be required to prepare and submit homework through LUNA before a given deadline.The instructor will be available for consultation through mail and LUNA to maximise students' understanding and performance.Students can also contact the instructor at any other time through the mail and/or LUNA.

第2回:
Core Week 1
Face-to-face 《90 minurtes》

Classical Japanese cinema: an overview of the Masters
Ozu, Mizoguchi, Naruse, Makino, Yamanaka, Gosho
Film Viewing: "Sisters of Gion" (Mizoguchi, 1936) or/ and "Naniwa Elegy" (Mizoguchi, 1936)
Images of Kyoto / Osaka in the films
Worksheet

A film by Ozu, Mizoguchi, Naruse, Makino, Yamanaka or Gosho.

Core Week 2
Face-to-face《90 minurtes》

Interwar modernism and social critique
Historical overview through different cinema clips and discussion
Cinema in transition: actress Takako Irie and her roles through time ( modern girl,"moga", Kurosawa's suffering war woman, "bakeneko henshin", cat monster transformation in horror films)
Literature in film: Tanizaki and Kawabata
Reading a short story by KawabataFollow instructions in LUNA's announcements shortly before our class meeting time and/or refer to previous announcements for tasks and homework.

トピック.授業外学習2:
Students will be required to prepare classes in advance, do homework, read about, and view different media examples relevant to the course contents.
Materials and online content and resources will be provided by the instructor during the course.
Students are required to get acquainted with the LUNA system to access class information, readings, relevant web links, and/ or submit homework
Homework for every class is compulsory and failure to present it in class will affect the final grade.

教科書/Required texts0: All class materials will be prepared and shared by the instructor via LUNA.
参考文献・資料/Reference books0:
The instructor will provide a list of useful bibliography and online resources at the beginning and throughout the course.
Reading some of this material is compulsory.
Students are required to get acquainted with the LUNA system to access class information and submit homework.
All other materials will be provided by the instructor through LUNA.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。
DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。
各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。

成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: Understanding of class material, ability to discuss this. Attendance and positive, active participation.
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note Test: 50%End-of-term Essay: 20%Tasks: 15%Field Trip Report: 15%
備考Note0:
Due to the extensive nature of the subject, many films or clips from films will have to be watched outside the class.
A series of compulsory readings, worksheets, and handouts will be assigned to prepare for the following class and must be read and prepared.
Students must write an essay on a film of their choice from any period or genre, including anime.
The instructor will provide more information about the requirements of the essay at the beginning of the course.
